anti_claim: Batch 7 station runtime capsule verifies exact copied non-secret station host, live-instrument, station-store, and source-test bodies only. It does not start or validate a hosted UI, authorize browser or operator-thread access, dispatch providers, mutate source, approve publication or release, claim private-root equivalence, or prove complete frontend coverage.

allowed_public_inputs: exact copied non-secret station runtime and frontend source modules
source refs, digests, line counts, and required anchors
stable negative-case ids and error codes
original vitest witness metadata without stdout bodies

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A fixture-only station smoke test, hosted UI acceptance claim, browser session claim, provider dispatch, source mutation, publication or release signal, private-root equivalence claim, or broad proof that all frontend station states are covered.
failure_modes: trace workbench host imports static runtime code before installing the boot probe
mission refresh timers disappear from the copied host runtime body
live agent instrument view model collapses unknown proof state into a known status
attention underfiring stops being an explicit visible status bucket
station store launches duplicate operations instead of enforcing single-flight behavior
station warming state stops retrying through bounded backoff
source module digest or line count drifts from the macro station source without a refreshed exact copy and manifest

receipts include copied body text, private refs, browser or HUD state, provider payload bodies, credentials, operator transcripts, source mutation authority, release authority, or live UI correctness overclaims
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe exact-copy station runtime capsule over the trace workbench host boot probe, live agent instrument view model, station store resilience FSM, and original source-test witnesses.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader rerun station runtime guardrails as provenance-backed substrate without needing the hosted UI, browser session, operator thread, provider dispatch, source mutation, publication, release, or private-root authority.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: require five copied station runtime or source-test modules
verify source-to-target digest equality, line counts, and required anchors for every copied source module
exercise trace workbench boot-probe ordering, live instrument proof and attention status handling, and station store single-flight retry behavior
require stable negative-case codes for missing boot probe, attention underfiring, unknown proof collapse, duplicate station launch, and missing warming retry

keep hosted UI runtime, browser or HUD state, operator-thread authority, provider dispatch, model dispatch, source mutation, publication, release, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness authority false
keep copied source bodies under source_modules only; receipts may expose refs, digests, anchors, counts, witness metadata, verdicts, and omission receipts but not body text

author.purpose: create a valid Batch 7 station runtime capsule instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Batch 7 station runtime capsule without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_batch7_station_runtime_capsule and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

anti_claims: Batch 7 station runtime capsule verifies exact copied non-secret station host, live-instrument, station-store, and source-test bodies only. It does not start or validate a hosted UI, authorize browser or operator-thread access, dispatch providers, mutate source, approve publication or release, claim private-root equivalence, or prove complete frontend coverage.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry accepted_public_runtime_standard plus standard-local validator_contract, receipt_contract, source_refs, authority_ceiling, public_private_boundary, and anti-claim floor.
